incidents.title: LiDAR inference latency spike after INT8 rollout
incidents.project: LiDAR Object Detection System for Autonomous Vehicles

timeline.event: Issue detected during prototype validation run.
Team correlated symptoms across affected services and deployment logs.
Mitigation applied on active rig and behavior partially recovered.
Incident downgraded after replay verification completed.

incidents.root_cause: TensorRT calibration buffers were retained across hot reloads, fragmenting GPU memory and stretching inference latency under replay-heavy sessions.
contributing_signals: GPU free memory drops after each engine reload even when frame rate is flat
latency spikes get worse after QA replays a calibration bundle twice
restarts temporarily recover p95 inference time
mitigation_steps: Rolled edge-inference-service back to previous FP16 build on jetson-xavier-edge-02.
Disabled automatic engine reload during replay-driven smoke tests.
Added allocator counter collection to telemetry dashboard for the next canary.

incidents.title: Tracking delay during dense replay with telemetry enabled
incidents.project: LiDAR Object Detection System for Autonomous Vehicles

timeline.event: Issue detected during prototype validation run.
Team correlated symptoms across affected services and deployment logs.
Mitigation applied on active rig and behavior partially recovered.
Incident downgraded after replay verification completed.

incidents.root_cause: A mismatched reliable QoS profile on the tracking subscriber combined with telemetry burst publishing caused message buildup during dense scenes.
contributing_signals: track age jumps after telemetry export starts on the same rig
backlog is higher on the Xavier device than the Orin dev box
reducing telemetry flush interval makes tracking delay less visible
mitigation_steps: Throttled telemetry exporter flush interval on replay rigs.
Switched tracking subscriber to best-effort profile on the demo branch.
Captured ROS2 callback timing traces from Xavier for follow-up analysis.

incidents.title: Lane offset after warm ECU restart on calibration branch
incidents.project: Lane Keeping Assist System using 2D Camera in ADAS ECU

timeline.event: Issue detected during prototype validation run.
Team correlated symptoms across affected services and deployment logs.
Mitigation applied on active rig and behavior partially recovered.
Incident downgraded after replay verification completed.

incidents.root_cause: The calibration service reused a stale extrinsic cache when the ECU restarted without a full profile flush, so lane inference mixed fresh frames with an old transform.
contributing_signals: lane center error is small after cold boot but grows after a warm restart
active calibration checksum looks correct while reported extrinsic age is stale
uploading the same bundle twice sometimes recenters the lane mask
mitigation_steps: Forced calibration cache flush after each uploaded bundle.
Restarted the camera-ingestion-service after profile activation on the rig.
Pinned lane tests to cold-boot setup until cache lifecycle was clarified.

incidents.title: Steering jitter on timing-profile-b during highway replay
incidents.project: Lane Keeping Assist System using 2D Camera in ADAS ECU

timeline.event: Issue detected during prototype validation run.
Team correlated symptoms across affected services and deployment logs.
Mitigation applied on active rig and behavior partially recovered.
Incident downgraded after replay verification completed.

incidents.root_cause: Controller smoothing consumed camera timestamps from a different clock domain than the CAN feedback path, amplifying oscillation once speed exceeded 70 km/h.
contributing_signals: steering oscillation starts only when the faster timing profile is enabled
diagnostics reports controller delta spikes without lane confidence collapsing
the issue is hard to reproduce in replay because wall clock pacing is different
mitigation_steps: Rolled controller timing-profile-b back to profile-a.
Disabled aggressive smoothing gains above 70 km/h in the prototype build.
Started logging both sensor and monotonic timestamps in diagnostics output.

incidents.title: Telemetry endpoint saturation hides LiDAR queue depth counters
incidents.project: LiDAR Object Detection System for Autonomous Vehicles

timeline.event: Issue detected during prototype validation run.
Team correlated symptoms across affected services and deployment logs.
Mitigation applied on active rig and behavior partially recovered.
Incident downgraded after replay verification completed.

incidents.root_cause: Local implementation regression uncovered during prototype iteration and resolved with a targeted rollback or patch.
contributing_signals: Symptom reproduced on the active branch.
Recent code change aligned with the failure window.
mitigation_steps: Reduced metric payload size for replay-only runs.
Restored queue depth field with fallback serialization.
